meta.creator: Christian Jackson (playlist summaries — technical terms only)
meta.purpose: Glossary for voice-command post-processing, training data, and future UI hints.

entries.summary: MA Lighting control platform; successor to grandMA2 with native software and dedicated hardware.
Dedicated mode for backward compatibility so existing MA2 shows can run during transition.
Software-only MA3; useful for pre-programming. macOS version cannot output DMX without parameter licensing constraints discussed in MA ecosystem.
Licensed output capacity; full-size consoles discussed with very large baseline counts (e.g. marketing figures like 250k parameters at high tier).
New multi-step effect model nested in cues; replaces purely table-based MA2 effects for many workflows.
Effect structure where individual steps can carry acceleration/deceleration and modulation concepts.
Selection view as a coordinate grid (rows/columns) for stepping fixtures and distributing phasers spatially.
Distinct ID classes (e.g. house lights vs video vs movers) so selections are not only generic fixture numbers.
Fixture description format; MA3 imports physical, 3D, and color-space data from manufacturer GDTF.
Idea of a temporary scratch region for quick sequences/macros without cluttering permanent pools.
Hardware layout changing attribute mapping and manipulation vs MA2.
Motorized faders with RGB feedback for color-coding playbacks/macros.
Grand Master implemented as rotary control rather than traditional fader on MA3 hardware.
Full-size MA3 hardware discussed with fewer physical X-keys vs MA2 (noted as tradeoff in coverage).
Console cooling design to reduce dust and noise vs PC-style case cooling on MA2.
Legacy wing noted as not unlocking MA3 parameters or running MA3 natively.
MA2 hardware can act as output nodes in MA3 session but does not expand licensed parameters.
Keys/workflow tying external visualizer use with relaxed parameter-unlock friction vs older constraints.
XT lacks items like internal UPS, built-in keyboard, letterboxed screens, dual command displays; Lite doubles parameter tier vs XT in discussed SKUs.
MA3 touchscreens are capacitive vs MA2 resistive (fingernail era).
USB 3 ports positioned on palm rests for reach while programming.
